title: Rain Over Blackwood Street
content:
Rain had a peculiar effect on Blackwood Street.

Whenever storms arrived, residents claimed the old cobblestones reflected shapes that disappeared as soon as the clouds passed. Visitors dismissed the stories as imagination until architect Daniel noticed unusual patterns himself.

He returned after several storms carrying old maps of the neighborhood.

As water filled the shallow grooves between the stones, forgotten outlines became visible beneath the reflections. They matched the foundations of buildings demolished more than a century earlier.

The street itself had preserved traces of the city's original design.

Generations of residents had unknowingly walked across history every day.

The mysterious reflections were not illusions but reminders that cities rarely erase their past completely. Sometimes history survives beneath modern streets, revealing itself only under the right conditions.

The rain had never created the mystery.

It merely revealed what had always been there.
